Setting.name: applicationName
Setting.label: Default Application
Setting.helpText: Default application shown in the plugin UI dropdown. The UI lets users pick any application at scan time.

Setting.name: emailAccountAttributes
Setting.label: Account Email Attributes
Setting.helpText: Candidate account attributes holding the email, matched to standard Identity.email. First non-empty wins.

Setting.name: loginAccountAttributes
Setting.label: Account Login Attributes
Setting.helpText: Candidate account attributes holding the login/username, matched to standard Identity.name. Falls back to the CN of the DN.

Setting.name: firstNameAccountAttributes
Setting.label: Account First-Name Attributes
Setting.helpText: Candidate account attributes holding the given/first name, matched to standard Identity.firstname.

Setting.name: lastNameAccountAttributes
Setting.label: Account Last-Name Attributes
Setting.helpText: Candidate account attributes holding the surname/last name, matched to standard Identity.lastname.

Setting.name: displayNameAccountAttributes
Setting.label: Account Display-Name Attributes
Setting.helpText: Candidate account attributes holding the display/full name, matched to standard Identity.displayName.

Setting.name: upnAccountAttributes
Setting.label: Account UPN Attributes
Setting.helpText: Candidate account attributes holding the UPN, matched against Identity.email (exact and local-part). First non-empty wins.

Setting.name: employeeIdAccountAttributes
Setting.label: Account Employee-ID Attributes
Setting.helpText: Candidate account attributes holding the employee ID/number. Only scored when an identity-side employee-ID attribute is also configured below.

Setting.name: departmentAccountAttributes
Setting.label: Account Department Attributes
Setting.helpText: Candidate account attributes holding the department (supporting signal). Leave blank to disable.

Setting.name: companyAccountAttributes
Setting.label: Account Company Attributes
Setting.helpText: Candidate account attributes holding the company/organisation (supporting signal). Leave blank to disable.

Setting.name: managerAccountAttributes
Setting.label: Account Manager Attributes
Setting.helpText: Candidate account attributes holding the manager name/email (supporting signal). Compared against the identity's manager.

Setting.name: serviceAccountPatterns
Setting.label: Service-Account Name Patterns
Setting.helpText: Comma-separated WHOLE-TOKEN patterns that flag non-human (service/bot) accounts. Applies a scoring PENALTY (-25). The login is split on non-alphanumeric boundaries and a pattern matches only a complete token (so 'sa' matches the 'sa' account, not 'sam'). Leave blank to disable.

Setting.name: adminAccountPatterns
Setting.label: Admin/Privileged Account Patterns
Setting.helpText: Comma-separated WHOLE-TOKEN patterns that flag privileged accounts. Applies a scoring PENALTY (-20). Leave blank to disable.

Setting.name: testAccountPatterns
Setting.label: Test-Account Name Patterns
Setting.helpText: Comma-separated WHOLE-TOKEN patterns that flag test / non-production accounts (separate from service accounts). Applies a lighter scoring PENALTY (-15) and labels the row as a test-account match. Leave blank to disable.

Setting.name: identityEmployeeIdAttribute
Setting.label: Identity Employee-ID Attribute
Setting.helpText: Name of the searchable Identity attribute holding the employee ID (e.g. employeeId). Strongest signal (weight 30). Blank = not used.

Setting.name: identityDepartmentAttribute
Setting.label: Identity Department Attribute
Setting.helpText: Name of the Identity attribute holding the department. Supporting signal. Blank = not used.

Setting.name: identityCompanyAttribute
Setting.label: Identity Company Attribute
Setting.helpText: Name of the Identity attribute holding the company/organisation. Supporting signal. Blank = not used.

Setting.name: candidateLimit
Setting.label: Max Candidates Per Account
Setting.helpText: Maximum candidate identities retrieved and scored per orphan account. Higher = more thorough but slower. Default 25.

Setting.name: matchThreshold
Setting.label: Match Threshold Score
Setting.helpText: Minimum final score (0-100) below which ambiguity is not flagged and a match is considered unreliable. Default 70.

Setting.name: ambiguityGap
Setting.label: Ambiguity Gap
Setting.helpText: If the best candidate beats the runner-up by fewer than this many points (and clears the threshold), the result is flagged ambiguous. Default 8.

Setting.name: maxScanResults
Setting.label: Max Accounts Per Scan
Setting.helpText: Maximum orphan accounts processed in a single SYNCHRONOUS scan. A scan runs in-request and fires several candidate queries per orphan, so a large value can time out the browser and pin a server thread. Default 1000; the UI warns when the cap is hit so you can narrow scope (e.g. by application). For very large populations, run scans off-hours or in smaller batches.
